description:
You are acting as a FinOps analyst who has been asked to tighten some cost-control configuration files.  
All work happens inside the directory:

    /home/user/finops

The folder (already present) contains two configuration files that you must **edit in-place**, and after you finish you must create one human-readable log file confirming the exact changes you made.  The automated grader will compare every byte of the final files, so follow the formatting requirements precisely.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1. /home/user/finops/cost-alerts.yaml  (YAML 1.2)

Initial contents:
```
alerts:
  - name: daily-spend
    threshold: 500
    channels:
      - slack
      - email
  - name: monthly-spend
    threshold: 15000
    channels:
      - slack
```

Required final contents:
```
alerts:
  - name: daily-spend
    threshold: 400
    channels:
      - slack
      - email
  - name: monthly-spend
    threshold: 15000
    channels:
      - slack
      - pagerduty
```
Notes:
• Only the integer value of threshold for “daily-spend” changes (from 500 → 400).  
• A single new entry “pagerduty” must be appended (exact order shown) under channels for “monthly-spend”.  
• Pay careful attention to indentation: two spaces per level, no tabs, no trailing whitespace.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2. /home/user/finops/budget.toml  (TOML v1.0.0)

Initial contents:
```
[budget]
monthly_limit = 20000
daily_limit  = 700

[metadata]
owner        = "finops-team"
last_updated = "2023-01-15"
```

Required final contents:
```
[budget]
monthly_limit = 18000
daily_limit  = 600

[metadata]
owner        = "finops-team"
last_updated = "2024-01-01"
```
Notes:
• Only the three values change; keys, section order, spacing and alignment must stay exactly as shown.  
• The two spaces before the “=” under `[budget]` and the eight spaces before the “=” under `[metadata]` must be preserved.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3. /home/user/finops/update_log.txt  (plain UTF-8 text)

You must create this new file.  It is a single-line-per-change audit trail in the exact order below, each line terminated by a single Unix newline (`\n`), **no extra blank line at the end**:

```
Cost Alert: daily-spend threshold changed: 500 -> 400
Cost Alert: monthly-spend channel added: pagerduty
Budget: monthly_limit changed: 20000 -> 18000
Budget: daily_limit changed: 700 -> 600
Budget: last_updated changed: 2023-01-15 -> 2024-01-01
```

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
What you have to do (≈3-4 terminal commands):
• Navigate to the directory.  
• Edit the two configuration files exactly as specified.  
• Create the update_log.txt file with the five audit lines shown.  
• Save all files so the grader can read them.

Do **not** move, rename, or delete any files other than performing the required edits and creating the single log file.  When you are done, the three files must match the “Required final contents” sections byte-for-byte.

Permissions: directory and files owned by user, default 644 for files, 755 for directory so the agent can write. No root needed.
